mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack reduces wage inflation pressures and signals weakening consumer spending, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive rank IC > 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in the industrial production index signals capacity utilization exhaustion and impending corporate margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes produce positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when isolated to this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and consumer credit growth, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >58% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods exhibiting this labor market configuration.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings for industrial and materials sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a sustained rise in the industrial production index confirms supply chain normalization and capacity rebuilding, which reduces input cost volatility and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases above 102 in recovery regimes yield negative 5-day forward returns in >65% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive power drops below statistical significance (p > 0.05).
